title: A Visual Guide to Exponential Decay
subtitle: A test article to verify JSX rendering with Recharts.

title: CatBoost: The Theoretically Principled Member of the Gradient Boosting Trinity
subtitle: Ordered boosting eliminates prediction shift. Ordered target statistics handle categorical features without leakage. A deep dive.

title: Conformal Prediction: Guaranteed Prediction Intervals for Any Model
subtitle: Distribution-free, finite-sample coverage guarantees. No Gaussianity. No Bayesian priors. Just valid intervals.

title: Explainable Boosting Machines: The Glass-Box That Rivals the Black Box
subtitle: As accurate as gradient boosting, as interpretable as linear regression. Microsoft's InterpretML flagship model.

title: The Practitioner's Deep Dive: Time Series Feature Selection in Practice
subtitle: Mechanics, intuition, diagnostics, and honest limits of 47 methods — with worked examples and how to tell genuine importance from statistical noise.

title: What Happens When You Shuffle a Feature? The Definitive Guide to PFI
subtitle: The simplest, most widely used, and most misunderstood tool in the ML interpretability toolkit. When it works, when it misleads, and what to use instead.

title: Beyond Point Forecasts: Prediction Intervals That Actually Work
subtitle: From 'demand will be 215' to 'demand will be 215 [195, 238] at 90% coverage.' Quantile regression, conformal, and native probabilistic models compared.

title: RAG in 2026: Still Essential, No Longer Default
subtitle: Long-context models can swallow 2 million tokens. Agents can search, verify, and reason. When is RAG still the right answer?

title: SAFE: Surrogate-Assisted Feature Extraction for Time Series
subtitle: Extracting monotonic, piecewise-constant feature transformations from black-box time series models using ALE profiles and surrogate trees.

title: Explaining Time Series Forecasts with Shapash: A Practitioner's Guide
subtitle: How to use MAIF's explainability library to make lag features, rolling statistics, and date features legible to stakeholders.

title: Splitwise in Python: The Complete Practitioner's Guide
subtitle: From OAuth handshakes to debt-minimisation graphs — everything a data engineer needs to wire Python into Splitwise's REST API.

title: The Equation That Decides: A Deep Dive into Stochastic Dynamic Programming
subtitle: In 1953, a RAND mathematician wrote an equation that became the heart of sequential decision-making — from inventory warehouses to AlphaGo.

title: The Bridge Between Time Series and Tabular ML: A Systematic Guide to Feature Engineering
subtitle: Lags, rolling statistics, Fourier transforms, calendar features, and the leakage traps that lurk in every time series pipeline.
